t.style.workflow_what: Determines how each frame's illustration is generated and its effect (e.g., using FLUX, SD models)
t.style.workflow_how: Place the exported image_xxx.json workflow file(API format) into the workflows/selfhost/ folder (for local ComfyUI) or the workflows/runninghub/ folder (for cloud)
t.style.video_workflow_what: Determines how each frame's video clip is generated and its effect (e.g., using different video generation models)
t.style.video_workflow_how: Place the exported video_xxx.json workflow file(API format) into the workflows/selfhost/ folder (for local ComfyUI) or the workflows/runninghub/ folder (for cloud)
t.style.prompt_prefix_what: Automatically added before all image prompts to control the illustration style uniformly (e.g., cartoon, realistic)
t.style.prompt_prefix_how: Enter style description in the input box below. To save permanently, edit the config.yaml file
t.style.prompt_prefix_placeholder: Enter style prefix (leave empty for config default)
t.style.prompt_prefix_help: This text will be automatically added before all image generation prompts. To permanently change, edit config.yaml
t.style.description_placeholder: Describe the illustration style you want (any language)...

t.template.separator_selected: Please select a specific template, not the group header
t.template.what: Controls the visual layout and design style of each frame (title, text, image arrangement)
t.template.how:
Place .html template files in templates/SIZE/ directories (e.g., templates/1080x1920/). Templates are automatically grouped by size. Custom CSS styles are supported.

**Template Naming Convention**

- `static_*.html` → Static style templates (no AI-generated media)
- `image_*.html` → Image generation templates (AI-generated images)
- `video_*.html` → Video generation templates (AI-generated videos)

**Note**

At least one of the following browsers must be installed on your computer for proper operation:
1. Google Chrome (Windows, macOS)
2. Chromium Browser (Linux)
3. Microsoft Edge
t.template.type.static_hint: Uses template's built-in styles, no AI-generated media required. You can customize background images and other parameters in the template.
t.template.type.image_hint: AI automatically generates illustrations matching the narration content. Image size is determined by the template.
t.template.type.video_hint: AI automatically generates video clips matching the narration content. Video size is determined by the template.

t.tts.what: Converts narration text to natural human-like speech (some workflows support reference audio for voice cloning)
t.tts.how: Voiceover engines are split into Edge TTS local synthesis, CosyVoice local model, and ComfyUI workflow synthesis. CosyVoice local model is installed under tools/cosyvoice/ and runs through its own Python environment without ComfyUI. ComfyUI workflow synthesis can use tts_xxx.json workflows from workflows/selfhost/ or workflows/runninghub/.

t.screen_recording.description: Recognize speech, refine industry subtitles, burn subtitles into recorded videos, and export editable CapCut materials
t.screen_recording.input.title: Recorded Video
t.screen_recording.input.caption: Enter a server-local video path, or upload terminology/correction Markdown files to improve ASR and subtitle cleanup.
t.screen_recording.input.video_path_help: Must be a local path accessible to the server running Pixelle-Video.
t.screen_recording.input.path_is_directory: The current path is a folder, not a video file: {path}. Enter a specific video file path, for example /Users/name/Movies/demo.mp4
t.screen_recording.input.glossary_help:
Use this to define standard business terms. Markdown tables are supported. Recommended columns: Term and Alias/Misrecognition.

Example:

| Term | Description | Alias/Misrecognition |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Work Order | Production task order | workorder, order |
| Recipe | Formula name | receipe, formula |
| Batch | Batch number | badge, lot |
| PLC | Controller | P L C, controller |

During processing, text in Alias/Misrecognition will be corrected to the standard Term.
t.screen_recording.input.correction_help:
Use this to fix words, pronunciations, acronyms, or polyphones that ASR often gets wrong. Markdown tables are supported. Recommended columns: Wrong Recognition and Correct Text.

Example:

| Wrong Recognition | Correct Text | Note |
| --- | --- | --- |
| restart up | restart | Spoken phrase cleanup |
| P two seven | P27 | Point number |
| Siemens S seven one thousand two | Siemens S7-1200 | Device model |
| mark down | Markdown | Technical term |

During processing, Wrong Recognition text will be replaced with Correct Text.
t.screen_recording.subtitle.title: Subtitle Recognition and Correction
t.screen_recording.subtitle.industry_conte: Example: industrial control screen recording with work orders, Recipe, Batch, device interlocks, alarm acknowledgement, and related terms
AI uses this context to clean subtitles so they match the industry vocabulary.
t.screen_recording.subtitle.ai_polish_help: Remove filler words, correct terminology, obvious slips, repeated modifiers, and awkward sentences so subtitles fit the video's industry context.
t.screen_recording.pace.gap_threshold_help: When the gap between adjacent speech segments exceeds this many seconds, smart compression cuts the blank portion.
t.screen_recording.pace.clip_padding_help: Seconds kept before and after each speech segment so cuts do not feel too abrupt.
t.screen_recording.tts.enable_help: Regenerate voiceover from cleaned subtitles. Original recording audio is not kept. Optional background music can be mixed with the new voiceover.
t.screen_recording.tts.preview_default: Hello, this is a test voice.
t.screen_recording.output.title: Output
t.screen_recording.output.need_video_path: Enter the local recorded video path first.
t.story_lesson.title: Story Title
t.story_lesson.title_placeholder: Example: The Fox and the Grapes, A Gift to Wang Lun, Nuwa Mends the Sky
t.story_lesson.title_help: Enter only the idiom, proverb, fable, myth, poem, textbook story, culture topic, or picture-book title
t.story_lesson.audience_help: Controls narration vocabulary, sentence length, explanation depth, pacing, and life examples. Picture books default to a younger audience.
t.story_lesson.duration_help: This is a planning target, not a hard cut. The system uses it to shape narration length and storyboard pacing. Longer topics need more time and scenes.
t.story_lesson.scenes_help: 6-8 scenes usually tell a complete story without dragging
t.story_lesson.include_example.textbook: Add a classroom or daily-life example
t.story_lesson.include_example.character: Add a school or family example
t.story_lesson.extra_requirements_placehol: Example: sound like a primary-school teacher; end with a thinking question; keep visuals gentle
t.story_lesson.prompt_prefix_help: Prepended to each scene's image/video prompt to unify style, characters, composition, or exclusions. You can leave it empty.
t.story_lesson.prompt_prefix_placeholder: Example: children's picture-book style, soft watercolor, bright and warm, consistent characters, clean frame, no text

t.asset_based.video_title_help: Main title for the video, leave empty to hide title
t.asset_based.intent_placeholder: e.g., Promote our pet store's year-end special offers to attract more customers, use a warm and friendly tone
t.asset_based.intent_help: Describe the purpose, message, and desired style of this video
t.asset_based.duration_help: Expected video duration, AI will adjust based on asset count

t.asset_based.source.how: LLM Vision Analysis understands images/videos; RunningHub Cloud Workflow and Local ComfyUI Workflow execute ComfyUI workflows; Alibaba Bailian / Tongyi Wanxiang runs DashScope media generation.

Fragrance leaves lasting memories. If you're looking for your own personalized fragrance, try TALIA. It is exquisite and profound, enhancing your charm. Create wonderful moments today with TALIA.

t.digital_human.goods_title_help: The product name. If provided, AI will generate narration automatically. You don’t need to enter the product description again unless needed—add it below if required.
t.digital_human.workflow.what: Configure the two-step workflow for digital human video generation: step 1 generates collage and script, step 2 generates final video

t.action_transfer.assets.video_what: Upload the reference video used for the migration action
t.action_transfer.assets.video_how: Supports MP4/MKV/MOV image formats. The reference video only supports single-person action migration. It is recommended that the video action be clearly displayed
t.action_transfer.assets.video_upload_help: Only supports single video uploads, and the video can only show one person. The video length must be less than or equal to 30 seconds. If the video length is longer than 30 seconds, only the first 30 seconds will be used as a reference
t.action_transfer.assets.video_empty_hint: 💡 Please upload a reference video for the action transfer

t.action_transfer.assets.image_how: Supports JPG/PNG/WebP and other image formats. Clear and relevant images are recommended

t.action_transfer.assets.image_upload_help: Single image uploads are supported only; images featuring a single person will produce the best results
t.action_transfer.assets.image_empty_hint: 💡 Please upload a reference video for motion transfer
t.action_transfer.input.topic_placeholder: Enter a creative prompt (e.g., imitate the dance in the reference video, keeping the position constant, with consistent and synchronized steps, etc.)

t.action_transfer.assets.image_warning: Upload an image for the transfer motion

t.pipeline.video_commentary.name: Movie Commentary
t.pipeline.video_commentary.description: Input source video path, AI auto-generates commentary video
t.commentary.what: Enter the source video path and AI will automatically analyze the plot, generate commentary script, synthesize voiceover, add captions and cover, and produce a commentary video in one click
t.commentary.how: Enter the absolute path of the local video file (e.g. /Users/name/movies/episode01.mp4). Larger videos take longer to process. It is recommended to use with subtitle files in the same directory
t.commentary.video_path_help: Enter the absolute path of the local video file, video file is not transferred
t.commentary.video_not_found: ❌ Video file does not exist, please check the path
t.commentary.slot_ratio_help: The proportion of time allocated to narration within each video segment. 0.9 means 90% narration, 10% visual transition. Higher = tighter pacing, lower = more visual breathing room.
t.commentary.content_start_help: Start time of usable story content in the source video. 0 = auto-detect (recommended). Manually set if the intro is not properly skipped.
t.commentary.content_end_help: End time of usable story content. 0 = auto-detect until video end. Manually set if credits are very long.
t.commentary.cover_headline_help: Main headline on the 3-second cover intro. Leave blank for AI auto-generation based on the story.
t.commentary.cover_question_help: Subtitle below the cover headline, usually a curiosity-triggering question. Leave blank for AI auto-generation.
t.commentary.summary:
📋 Configuration Summary
- Source: {path}
- Target Duration: {duration} sec
- AI auto-generates commentary script, cover, captions and progress bar
t.commentary.video_path_hint: 💡 Please enter the source video file path
t.commentary.detected_embedded: ✅ Embedded subtitle stream detected ({count} streams, codecs: {codecs})
t.commentary.hard_subtitle_warning: 💡 Note: Hard subtitles may exist at the bottom of the frame (cannot be auto-detected). If confirmed, enable the 'Mask Original Subtitles' option below.
t.commentary.mask_subtitles_help: Apply blur + semi-transparent black mask to the bottom of the frame to cover original hard subtitles
t.commentary.mask_subtitle_height_ratio_he: The ratio of mask area to total frame height. If original subtitles are not fully covered, increase this value (e.g., 15%~25%)
t.commentary.segment_count_help: Split the source into multiple independent commentary videos. Each gets its own cover, script, and progress bar. E.g., 3 videos × 10min each = 30min total
t.commentary.bgm_not_selected: ⚠️ No BGM selected, will use silent mode (voiceover only)
t.commentary.original_audio_hint: Original video audio as background at {volume} volume
t.selfhost.warning.title: You have selected a Local ComfyUI workflow. Please confirm the following:
t.selfhost.warning.message:
1. First, **load and run** `{workflow_path}` workflow in your ComfyUI environment ({comfyui_url})
2. Ensure the workflow **runs successfully** without missing nodes or models
3. After the workflow is verified, return to this project to use it
t.selfhost.warning.hint: ⚠️ If you haven't verified this workflow in ComfyUI, subsequent runs **will definitely fail** (usually 400 error)! Do not skip this step.

t.commentary.jianying.title: 🎞️ Jianying Material Export
t.commentary.jianying.enable: Export Jianying material package (video clips / audio / subtitles / scripts)
t.commentary.jianying.help: After generation, automatically organize video clips, voiceover audio, subtitle files, cover assets, and narration scripts into the materials/ directory for easy import into Jianying for secondary editing. Large files use symlinks to save disk space.
t.commentary.jianying.import_hint: 💡 Open Jianying → Click Import → Select the corresponding folder in the material package
t.settings.bilibili.hint: Cookie path is configured per-user in the generation page, supporting multiple accounts
t.commentary.bilibili.cookie_upload_help: Upload cookies.json generated on your computer. Server will use it to upload videos to your account.
t.commentary.bilibili.cookie_path_local_he: If cookie file is already on the server, enter its path directly
t.commentary.bilibili.cookie_step1: Step 1: Get Cookie on your computer (expand for details)
t.commentary.bilibili.cookie_step1_desc:
Follow these steps on your local computer terminal:

1. **Download biliup** (matching your OS — click the code block above to copy)
2. **Run login command**: Follow prompts to enter phone number and verification code
3. **Get cookies.json**: After successful login,  will be generated in the current directory

Then proceed to Step 2 to upload the file.
t.commentary.bilibili.cookie_step1_tip: 💡 Tip: If you are on the server machine directly, skip upload and enter the cookie path below
t.commentary.bilibili.cookie_guide_btn: 📖 How to get Cookie (click to toggle)
